methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Persona AI is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Nic Radford and Jerry Pratt that has raised $25M to date. The broader robotics and manufacturing sector comprises 1 public comparable and 8 private peers including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics. However, the narrow peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables meeting minimum thresholds, limiting reliance on median peer multiples.

The valuation analysis applies stage-progression methodology, which uses a 2.5x multiplier on total capital raised as a proxy for current valuation. This method is appropriate for seed-stage companies lacking robust revenue or profitability metrics. Applied to Persona AI's $25M raised, the stage-progression approach implies a $62.5M valuation. This reflects typical step-ups observed in seed-to-next-stage financing rounds within the sector.

Given methodological constraints, the analysis supports a valuation range of $53M to $72M, with a median estimate of $62M. This range reflects approximately ±15% variation around the stage-progression implied value, accounting for execution risk, IP defensibility, and timing to next capital event. The primary limitation remains the absence of directly comparable seed-stage robotics companies with sufficient transparency to validate the 2.5x multiplier empirically. Key uncertainties include capital efficiency, time to significant revenue milestones, and competitive positioning relative to the named peer group.
